_doc: Hooks are shell commands executed by Claude Code at lifecycle events. These are project-level safety nets. See https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks for the canonical spec.

preToolUse.command: echo 'Refusing to force-push from a hook. If you really mean it, run the command yourself.' >&2 && exit 2
echo 'Refusing rm -rf on root paths.' >&2 && exit 2
echo 'Reading .env files is denied by .claude/settings.json — this hook is a backup belt + braces.' >&2 && exit 0

postToolUse.command: if [ -f "$CLAUDE_TOOL_FILE_PATH" ] && [ "$(stat -c '%a' "$CLAUDE_TOOL_FILE_PATH")" != '600' ]; then echo 'Warning: file in tmp/ does not have mode 600 — keypair files should always be 600. Run: chmod 600 "'"$CLAUDE_TOOL_FILE_PATH"'"' >&2; fi

stop.command: if [ -n "$(git status --short 2>/dev/null | grep -E '^\?\?.*\.(env|json)$' | grep -v node_modules)' ]; then echo 'Note: untracked .env or .json files present — confirm none are sensitive before committing.' >&2; fi
